_meta.description: Peak heat loss from mechanical ventilation at design conditions per CSA F280-12 Section 5.2.6. Accounts for heat recovery from HRV/ERV systems using ATRE (Apparent Temperature Recovery Efficiency). Converts the annual ventilation heat loss from VentilationNodes to peak instantaneous value.

output.note: Peak instantaneous ventilation heat loss at design conditions, net of recovery

preconditions.description: Recovery efficiency must be between 0% and 100%

invariants.description: Net ventilation heat loss cannot be negative (recovery cannot exceed gross)

behavior.note: 1.21 = volumetric heat capacity of air (W·s/L·K). Net loss accounts for heat recovery.
100% recovery means no net ventilation heat loss
No ventilation means no ventilation heat loss

rationale.conversionFromAnnual: The annual formula in VentilationNodes d_121 is: grossLoss = (1.21 × ventRate × HDD × 24) / 1000 (kWh/yr). The F280 peak formula: peakGross = 1.21 × ventRate × deltaT (Watts). Net = peakGross × (1 - efficiency/100).
rationale.atre: ATRE (Apparent Temperature Recovery Efficiency) is the efficiency of the HRV/ERV at recovering heat from exhaust air. CSA F280-12 requires using the rated ATRE at -25 degC for heating design calculations.

where.T_design: Outdoor heating design temperature (degC) from d_23

formula.legacy: New peak calculation derived from VentilationNodes.js d_121/h_121 annual formulas

tests.name: Standard ventilation with 75% HRV, Toronto winter

references.source: CSA F280-12 Section 5.2.6 - Ventilation Heat Loss
CSA F280-12 Clause on ATRE at -25 degC rating conditions
